title: Robinia luxurians

taxonomy: Robinia luxurians is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes or pea family. It is a member of the genus Robinia, which consists of several species of trees and shrubs. Robinia luxurians is native to China and is also known as the Chinese locust tree or fragrant locust tree. This plant is highly valued for its fragrant flowers and is often cultivated in gardens and parks. Robinia luxurians is also known for its ability to fix nitrogen in the soil, making it a useful plant for soil conservation.
